commands.review_summary:
The PR tightens package-update restart verification so a healthy surviving gateway is not accepted unless managed service supervision is also verified, and adds unit plus GitHub Actions systemd proof coverage.

PR surface: Source +90, Tests +85, Config +97, Other +145. Total +417 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is high confidence: current main accepts the expected-version health probe without supervision proof, and the PR adds a regression test for the healthy-gateway/missing-supervisor state. I did not run the test locally because this review is read-only, but the linked fork proof also exercises the real systemd path after the patch.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No narrow automation repair remains; maintainers need to accept the compatibility-sensitive updater behavior and new proof workflow before merge.

target.title: fix(update): require service supervision after package refresh
target.body:
Fixes #90930

## Summary

This PR hardens the updater path after a package refresh so a surviving gateway process is not treated as sufficient proof that the managed service is still supervised.

The bug in #90930 is the health-only shortcut after `openclaw update`: if the gateway process remains alive, the updater can skip reinstalling/revalidating the managed service even when the package refresh removed the service unit / launch entry. That leaves the gateway alive only until the next crash, reboot, or restart, after which OpenClaw is no longer supervised.

## What changed

- Add a focused regression test that reproduces the post-refresh missing-supervisor case.
- Split process liveness from managed-service supervision proof.
- After a package refresh, require explicit supervisor validation/reinstall before accepting the update as complete.
- Preserve the existing quick path only for cases where the managed service remains provably installed and active.

## Evidence before the fix

Canonical issue evidence is in #90930, including the full packet in:

- https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/90930#issuecomment-4639636487

A test-only branch reproduced the failure before the fix:

- `test(update): reproduce missing supervisor after update`
- failing GitHub Actions run: https://github.com/snowzlm/openclaw/actions/runs/27064784784

That run demonstrates the current main behavior: a healthy gateway process can satisfy the post-refresh check even when the managed service proof is missing.

## Evidence after the fix

Prepared fix branch:

- `snowzlm:fix/90930-update-systemd-supervision`
- current head: `bd92afeb7c`

Validation performed on the focused patch scope:

- Focused updater regression coverage was updated for the missing-service-unit/live-gateway case.
- Existing Docker `update-migration` lane behavior is preserved; the additional real proof lives in a dedicated systemd workflow.
- GitHub Actions real Linux systemd proof passed for both the intact service path and the missing-supervisor path.

## Real behavior proof

Latest proof was run on GitHub-hosted Ubuntu runners using a real `systemctl --user` service, not the Docker systemctl shim. The workflow builds a package tarball from this PR head, installs it as a real npm global package, installs/starts `openclaw-gateway.service`, runs the package update path, and captures systemd, update, health, and readiness artifacts.

Current proof head:

- branch: `snowzlm:fix/90930-update-systemd-supervision`
- head: `bd92afeb7c`
- workflow: `Systemd Update Proof`
- run: https://github.com/snowzlm/openclaw/actions/runs/27091217405

Scenarios verified:

1. `intact-systemd-update` — normal managed-service package refresh
   - job: https://github.com/snowzlm/openclaw/actions/runs/27091217405/job/79955059168
   - result: passed
   - summary artifact: `status=passed`, `unit=openclaw-gateway.service`, `port=19879`
   - post-update systemd state:
     - `LoadState=loaded`
     - `ActiveState=active`
     - `SubState=running`
     - `FragmentPath=<runner-home>/.config/systemd/user/openclaw-gateway.service`
   - post-update probes:
     - `/healthz`: `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}`
     - `/readyz`: `"ready": true`

2. `missing-supervisor-systemd-update` — service unit removed while the gateway process remains live
   - job: https://github.com/snowzlm/openclaw/actions/runs/27091217405/job/79955059165
   - result: passed
   - setup proof before update: systemd reported the service unit missing while the gateway was still live
   - package-update command completed with `status=ok`
   - post-update systemd state after repair/restart:
     - `LoadState=loaded`
     - `ActiveState=active`
     - `SubState=running`
     - `FragmentPath=<runner-home>/.config/systemd/user/openclaw-gateway.service`
   - post-update probes:
     - `/healthz`: `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}`
     - `/readyz`: `"ready": true`

This directly covers the requested real Linux systemd proof: a live gateway process is no longer treated as sufficient when the supervisor unit is missing; the update path repairs/revalidates the managed service and ends with a loaded, active, running systemd user unit.

## Risk

Low-to-medium. The change is intentionally fail-closed after package refresh: if service supervision cannot be proven, the updater repairs/revalidates the managed service instead of trusting only an already-running gateway process.

The tradeoff is an extra managed-service check in the update completion path, but it prevents the worse state where the process is temporarily alive while the boot/restart supervisor is gone.

## Rollback

Revert this PR to restore the previous health-only shortcut behavior after package refresh.
